title: Incremental State Machines, Concurrency, Cancellation, and Ownership · Runtime Contract: State Invariants
summary: A parser is recursive and side-effectful, multiple goroutines read one connection, cancellation races with response writes, and error paths cannot state which component owns the buffer or close. Planned artifact: Add and verify the state invariants increment in explicit protocol state, event, invariant, and ownership model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define invariants for cursor bounds, monotonic consumption, one framing choice, one response order, one close owner, and no effects before validation.
Represent protocol phase, required input, transition, emitted event, retained buffer, limit, and terminal result explicitly across fragmented reads.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict State Invariants · Theory State Invariants
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 8966 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce cursor, framing, reader, and terminal ownership invariants; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the State Invariants contract for the supplied data.
